meta.title: Unit 3 — Pronouns & I/me (Test 10)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Final mastery test: all pronoun types and I vs. me in varied contexts.

questions.question: A pronoun is a word that ___.
questions.explanation: A pronoun replaces a noun to avoid repetition.

questions.question: The pronoun "I" is always written as a capital letter.
questions.explanation: Yes! The pronoun "I" is ALWAYS capitalized — no exceptions.

questions.question: Tom likes to play basketball. → ___ likes to play basketball.
questions.explanation: Tom is male → use 'He'.

questions.question: "Me am happy" is a grammatically correct sentence.
questions.explanation: 'Me' cannot be the subject. Correct: "I am happy."

questions.question: Lisa and Amy are best friends. → ___ are best friends.
questions.explanation: Lisa and Amy = two people → use 'They'.

questions.question: Fill in: ___ am Oliver the otter. (I or me)
questions.hint: Subject of the sentence — comes before the verb
questions.explanation: 'I' is the subject — it goes before the verb 'am'. I am Oliver the otter.

questions.question: The bird is very small. → ___ is very small.
questions.explanation: "The bird" is an animal (gender not specified) → use 'It'.

questions.question: Sort these nouns: which pronoun replaces each one?
questions.explanation: He = male. They = plural. It = animal/thing. She = female.

questions.question: In the sentence "Can you give me the pencil?," 'me' is correctly used after the verb.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'me' comes after the verb 'give' as the object. This is correct.

questions.question: Fill in: Rosa gives ___ the basket. (I or me)
questions.explanation: 'me' is the object — it comes after the verb. Rosa gives me the basket.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "Can you give me the pencil?" 'me' is the object.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'I' correctly?
questions.explanation: "I love dance" — 'I' is the subject before the verb 'love'. The others need 'me'.

questions.question: The sentence "She always helps me with homework" is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'me' comes after the verb 'helps' (object). This is correct.

questions.question: The students are in the classroom. → ___ are in the classroom.
questions.explanation: "The students" is plural → use 'They'.

questions.question: Fill in: ___ live in the ranch. (I or me)
questions.explanation: 'I' is the subject — it goes before the verb. I live in the ranch.

questions.question: Sort these: which blank needs 'I', and which needs 'me'?
questions.explanation: 'I' = subject (before verb). 'me' = object (after verb).

questions.question: Which sentence has a mistake?
questions.explanation: "roars at I" is wrong — after a preposition, use 'me': "The lion roars at me."

questions.question: In the sentence "Mommy and I put our clothes in it," all pronouns are used correctly.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'I' = subject in 'Mommy and I'. 'it' = object pronoun replacing the basket. Both correct.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "I am not scared of the lion." 'I' is the subject.

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses the pronoun 'it'?
options: Mina is surprised. It is surprised.
The book is on the desk. It is on the desk.
Andy is kind. It is kind.
We love dogs. It loves dogs.
questions.explanation: "The book" is a thing → replace with 'It'. The others have errors (Mina/Andy = person, not 'it').
